title: Wendy App Configuration
description: Configuration file for Wendy edge computing applications (wendy.json).

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor support.

appId.description: Unique application identifier. Only letters, digits, '.', '_', and '-' are allowed.

platform.description: Target platform: linux (default Linux edge devices), wendyos (compatibility alias for linux), wendy-lite (ESP32 WASM), or darwin (native macOS via Wendy for Mac).

entitlements.description: Capabilities the app requires (GPU, network, Bluetooth, etc.).

files.description: Files or directories to sync to the device before the app starts.

brewfile.description: Optional Homebrew Bundle manifest for native Darwin deployments, relative to wendy.json. If omitted, a project-root Brewfile.wendy is auto-detected for native Mac runs.

isolation.description: Namespace isolation mode for multi-container deployments (e.g. shared-ipc, shared-network, isolated).

services.description: Per-service build and runtime configuration for a multi-service app.

entitlement.description: A single app entitlement granting access to a device capability.

mode.description: Network mode. "host" shares the host network stack; "none" disables networking.

oneOf.description: Deprecated: use the camera entitlement instead.
GPU access for AI inference or compute.

name.description: App ID — a shared namespace. Apps with the same ID can access each other's data.

oneOf.description: Persistent storage that survives container restarts.

mode.description: Camera access mode. Accepted for compatibility with the deprecated video entitlement.

allowlist.description: Allowed camera device paths. Accepted for compatibility with the deprecated video entitlement.

oneOf.description: Camera/V4L2 device access. Legacy video entitlement keys remain accepted for compatibility.

pins.description: GPIO pin numbers. Omit to grant access to all GPIO chips.

oneOf.description: HID input device access (barcode scanners, keyboards, etc.).

device.description: USB serial tty node name, e.g. "ttyACM0" or "ttyUSB0" (bare name, not a path). USB-only; on-board UARTs (ttyAMA, ttyS) are not supported.

oneOf.description: Serial tty device access (USB-attached servo buses, sensors, microcontrollers).

readiness.description: Probe configuration the CLI uses to determine when the app is ready.

tcpSocket.description: Check readiness by dialing a TCP port.

hookCommand.description: Commands to run at a lifecycle stage.

openURL.description: URL to open in the developer's default browser. Cross-platform; prefer this over a `cli` shell invocation of `open`/`xdg-open`/`start`. Supports ${WENDY_HOSTNAME} and ${WENDY_APP_ID} expansion.

cli.description: Command to run on the developer's machine. Note: `open`/`xdg-open`/`start` are not portable across macOS/Linux/Windows — use `openURL` to open a URL instead.

agent.description: Command to run on the device.

fileSyncEntry.description: A file or directory to sync to the device's app working directory.

path.description: Source path relative to wendy.json (file or directory).

to.description: Destination path relative to the app working directory. Defaults to path with any leading ./ stripped.

sourceRoot.description: Path to the Python source root directory.

run.description: Runtime configuration applied when the app is started.

args.description: Command-line arguments passed directly to the app executable.

ros2.description: ROS 2 runtime configuration injected into the container.

domainId.description: ROS_DOMAIN_ID. When omitted, a stable hash of appId in [0,232] is used.

rmw.description: RMW middleware. Short names or full identifiers. Defaults to rmw_cyclonedds_cpp.

distro.description: ROS 2 distribution (e.g. humble, jazzy). Defaults to humble.

service.description: A single service within a multi-service app.

context.description: Build context directory relative to wendy.json.

dependsOn.description: Service names this service starts after.

resourceLimits.description: Per-app or per-service resource ceilings enforced by the agent via cgroups. Omitted fields leave that resource unbounded.

memory.description: Hard memory limit. A number of bytes, optionally with a binary (Ki, Mi, Gi, Ti) or decimal (K, M, G, T) suffix, e.g. "512Mi". The container is OOM-killed if it exceeds this.

cpus.description: Maximum number of CPU cores as a decimal, e.g. "0.5", "1.5", "2". Enforced as a CFS quota over a 100ms period.

pids.description: Maximum number of processes/threads the container may create. Guards against fork bombs. Omit to use the agent's conservative default cap.
